habitat: Temperate Europe and Western Asia; roadsides, fields, waste ground; widely cultivated.
traditionalUsesEn: Mild digestive complaints, nervous restlessness, mild inflammatory conditions of the mouth and throat; external use for skin irritation.
traditionalUsesDe: Leichte Magen-Darm-Beschwerden, nervöse Unruhe, leichte Entzündungen im Mund- und Rachenraum; äußerlich bei Hautreizungen.
preparation: Infusion (tea), steam inhalation, compresses, baths; essential oil in diluted topical preparations.
cautions: Rare allergic reactions in people sensitive to Asteraceae (daisy family). Not a substitute for medical care in serious illness.
historicalNotes: Documented in European monastic herbals; German Commission E monographs support several traditional uses.
habitatDe: Gemäßigt Europa und Westasien; Wegränder, Äcker, Ödland; weit verbreitet angebaut.
preparationDe: Aufguss (Tee), Dampfinhalation, Umschläge, Bäder; ätherisches Öl nur in verdünnten äußerlichen Zubereitungen.
cautionsDe: Selten allergische Reaktionen in Personen empfindlich zu Asteraceae (Korbblütler). Kein Ersatz für medizinische Versorgung in schwere Erkrankung.
historicalNotesDe: Documented in Europaan monastic herbals; German Commission E monographs support several traditionell uses.

habitat: Sunny grasslands, roadsides, and open woods of Europe and Western Asia; naturalized elsewhere.
traditionalUsesEn: Mild depressive episodes and temporary nervous exhaustion (traditional herbal use); topical oil for minor wounds and bruises.
traditionalUsesDe: Leichte depressive Verstimmungen und vorübergehende nervöse Erschöpfung (traditionelle Anwendung); äußerlich als Öl bei leichten Wunden und Prellungen.
preparation: Dried herb tea or standardized extracts (oral); red oil (Oleum Hyperici) for external use.
cautions: Important drug interactions (e.g. with oral contraceptives, immunosuppressants, anticoagulants, some antidepressants) via CYP3A4 induction. Photosensitivity possible. Medical advice required before use.
historicalNotes: Named for flowering around St. John's Day (24 June); long history in European folk medicine.
habitatDe: Sunny grasslands, Wegränder, und lichte Wälder von Europa und Westasien; eingebürgert elsewhere.
preparationDe: Tee aus getrocknetem Kraut oder standardisierte Extrakte (oral); Rotöl (Oleum Hyperici) für die äußerliche Anwendung.
cautionsDe: Wichtig Arzneimittelwechselwirkungen (e.g. mit oral contraceptives, immunosuppressants, anticoagulants, some antidepressants) via CYP3A4 induction. Photosensibilität möglich. Ärztlicher Rat erforderlich vor der Anwendung.
historicalNotesDe: Named für flowering around St. John's Day (24 June); long history in Europaan folk medicine.

habitat: Mediterranean dry, rocky slopes; extensively cultivated in Provence (France) and elsewhere.
traditionalUsesEn: Mild anxiety, restlessness, and sleep disturbance; topical use for minor muscle tension; fragrance and insect-repellent traditions.
traditionalUsesDe: Leichte Angstzustände, Unruhe und Einschlafprobleme; äußerlich bei leichten Muskelverspannungen; Duft- und insektenabwehrende Tradition.
activeCompounds: Essential oil (linalool, linalyl acetate), tannins, coumarins
preparation: Dried flower tea, essential oil (aromatherapy, diluted topical), pillows and sachets.
cautions: Essential oil must be diluted for skin use; internal use of concentrated oil only under professional guidance.
historicalNotes: Cultivated since antiquity around the Mediterranean; central to French perfume and herbal traditions.
habitatDe: Mittelmeergebiet dry, Felsenhänge; extensively angebaut in Provence (Frankreich) und elsewhere.
activeCompoundsDe: Ätherisches Öl (linalool, linalyl acetate), tannins, coumarins
preparationDe: Tee aus getrockneten Blüten, ätherisches Öl (Aromapflege, verdünnt äußerlich), Duftkissen und Duftsäckchen.
cautionsDe: Ätherisches Öl muss für die Hautanwendung verdünnt werden; innere Anwendung von konzentriertem Öl nur unter fachlicher Anleitung.
historicalNotesDe: Angebaut since antiquity around die Mittelmeergebiet; central zu French perfume und herbal traditions.

habitat: Native to the Mediterranean; dry rocky soils; widely cultivated as culinary and medicinal herb.
traditionalUsesEn: Inflammation of the mouth and throat (gargle); excessive sweating; digestive complaints; culinary seasoning.
traditionalUsesDe: Entzündungen im Mund- und Rachenraum (Gurgeln); übermäßiges Schwitzen; Verdauungsbeschwerden; Küchengewürz.
activeCompounds: Essential oil (thujone, camphor, 1,8-cineole), rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, tannins
preparation: Leaf infusion for gargling or drinking; culinary use; tinctures.
cautions: High doses or prolonged use of thujone-rich preparations not recommended; avoid concentrated essential oil internally; caution in pregnancy.
historicalNotes: Name from Latin salvare (to save/heal); staple of Mediterranean monastic gardens.
habitatDe: Heimisch in die Mittelmeergebiet; dry rocky soils; weit verbreitet angebaut as kulinarisch und medicinal Kraut.
activeCompoundsDe: Ätherisches Öl (thujone, camphor, 1,8-cineole), rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, tannins
preparationDe: Blattaufguss zum Gurgeln oder Trinken; kulinarische Verwendung; Tinkturen.
cautionsDe: Hohe Dosen oder längere Anwendung von thujone-rich preparations nicht empfohlen; vermeiden konzentriert ätherisches Öl internally; caution in Schwangerschaft.
historicalNotesDe: Name von Latin salvare (to save/heal); staple von Mittelmeergebiet monastic gardens.

habitat: Coastal Mediterranean scrub (maquis); rocky, sunny, well-drained sites.
traditionalUsesEn: Digestive complaints, mild circulatory stimulation (traditional), topical use for muscle and joint discomfort; culinary herb.
traditionalUsesDe: Verdauungsbeschwerden, traditionell milde Kreislaufanregung, äußerlich bei Muskel- und Gelenkbeschwerden; Küchenkraut.
activeCompounds: Essential oil (1,8-cineole, camphor, α-pinene), rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, flavonoids
preparation: Leaf tea, culinary seasoning, diluted essential oil or ointments for external use.
cautions: Essential oil not for undiluted skin use or internal self-medication; high doses may irritate.
historicalNotes: Symbol of remembrance in European tradition; reclassified from Rosmarinus into Salvia based on molecular phylogeny.
habitatDe: Küstennah Mittelmeergebiet scrub (maquis); rocky, sunny, gut drainiert sites.
activeCompoundsDe: Ätherisches Öl (1,8-cineole, camphor, α-pinene), rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, flavonoids
preparationDe: Blatttee, kulinarische Würze, verdünntes ätherisches Öl oder Salben für die äußerliche Anwendung.
cautionsDe: Ätherisches Öl nicht unverdünnt Hautanwendung oder innerlich Selbstmedikation; hohe Dosen kann irritate.
historicalNotesDe: Symbol von remembrance in Europaan tradition; reclassified von Rosmarinus into Salvia based on molecular phylogeny.

habitat: Damp meadows, stream banks, and woodland edges of Europe and Western Asia.
traditionalUsesEn: Relief of mild nervous tension and sleep difficulty associated with restlessness.
traditionalUsesDe: Linderung leichter nervöser Anspannung und Einschlafschwierigkeiten bei Unruhe.
activeCompounds: Valerenic acid and derivatives, valepotriates (in fresh material), essential oil, GABA-related constituents
preparation: Dried root tea, tincture, standardized extracts (capsules/tablets).
cautions: May cause drowsiness; avoid combining with sedatives or alcohol without advice. Characteristic strong odor of dried root.
historicalNotes: Used since classical antiquity; listed in European pharmacopoeias for mild sleep and stress support.
habitatDe: Damp Wiesen, Bachufer, und Waldlund edges von Europa und Westasien.
activeCompoundsDe: Valerensäure und Derivate, Valepotriate (in frischem Material), ätherisches Öl, GABA-bezogene Inhaltsstoffe
preparationDe: Tee aus getrockneter Wurzel, Tinktur, standardisierte Extrakte (Kapseln/Tabletten).
cautionsDe: Kann Schläfrigkeit verursachen; Kombination mit Beruhigungsmitteln oder Alkohol ohne Beratung vermeiden. Charakteristischer starker Geruch der getrockneten Wurzel.
historicalNotesDe: Used since classical antiquity; listed in Europaan pharmacopoeias für mild sleep und stress support.

partsUsed: Aerial parts (herba), especially leaves and flowering tops
habitat: Dry, rocky Mediterranean hillsides; widely cultivated.
traditionalUsesEn: Productive coughs and colds (traditional expectorant), oral inflammation, digestive complaints; culinary herb.
traditionalUsesDe: Produktiver Husten und Erkältungen (traditionell schleimlösend), Entzündungen im Mundraum, Verdauungsbeschwerden; Küchenkraut.
preparation: Herb tea, syrups, gargles; culinary use; diluted essential oil in external products.
cautions: Essential oil is potent and must be diluted; not for undiluted internal use. High thymol content can irritate mucous membranes.
historicalNotes: Ancient Greek and Roman culinary and medicinal herb; thymol remains a well-studied monoterpene phenol.
partsUsedDe: Kraut (herba), especially Blätter und blühende Spitzen
habitatDe: Dry, rocky Mittelmeergebiet Hänge; weit verbreitet angebaut.
preparationDe: Krauttee, Sirupe, Gurgellösungen; kulinarische Verwendung; verdünntes ätherisches Öl in äußerlichen Produkten.
cautionsDe: Ätherisches Öl is potent und muss verdünnt werden; nicht unverdünnt innere Anwendung. High thymol content kann irritate mucous membranes.
historicalNotesDe: Ancient Greek und Roman kulinarisch und medicinal Kraut; thymol remains a well-studied monoterpene phenol.

habitat: Hybrid of Mentha aquatica and Mentha spicata; cultivated worldwide in temperate regions (notably England historically).
traditionalUsesEn: Digestive discomfort (flatulence, mild spasms), tension-type headache (topical oil diluted), oral freshness.
traditionalUsesDe: Verdauungsbeschwerden (Blähungen, leichte Krämpfe), Spannungskopfschmerz (verdünntes Öl äußerlich), Mundfrische.
activeCompounds: Essential oil (menthol, menthone), flavonoids, phenolic acids
preparation: Leaf tea, enteric-coated oil capsules for IBS-related symptoms (under product guidance), diluted topical oil.
cautions: May worsen reflux in some people. Essential oil not for infants/young children (risk of laryngeal spasm). Enteric-coated preparations have specific use guidance.
historicalNotes: Cultivated since at least the 17th century in England (e.g. Mitcham); one of the most widely used culinary and medicinal mints.
habitatDe: Hybrid von Mentha aquatica und Mentha spicata; angebaut worldwide in gemäßigt regions (notably Englund historically).
activeCompoundsDe: Ätherisches Öl (menthol, menthone), flavonoids, phenolic acids
preparationDe: Blatttee, magensaftresistente Öl-Kapseln bei Reizdarm-Beschwerden (nach Produktanleitung), verdünntes Öl äußerlich.
cautionsDe: May worsen reflux in some Personen. Ätherisches Öl not für Säuglinge/young Kinder (risk von laryngeal spasm). Enteric-coated preparations have specific use Anleitung.
historicalNotesDe: Angebaut since at least die 17th century in Englund (e.g. Mitcham); one von die most widely used kulinarisch und medicinal mints.

habitat: Tropical Asia origin; requires warm, humid climate; major production in India, China, and Southeast Asia.
traditionalUsesEn: Nausea and motion sickness, digestive sluggishness, cold-related complaints in traditional systems (Ayurveda, TCM culinary/medicinal use).
traditionalUsesDe: Übelkeit und Reisekrankheit, träge Verdauung, erkältungsbedingte Beschwerden in traditionellen Systemen (Ayurveda, TCM).
preparation: Fresh or dried rhizome tea, candied ginger, powdered spice, standardized extracts.
cautions: Generally recognized as safe as food; high medicinal doses may interact with anticoagulants—seek advice if on blood thinners.
floweringSeason: Rarely flowers in cultivation outside tropics
historicalNotes: Traded along ancient spice routes; among the earliest documented Asian medicinal spices in European records.
habitatDe: Tropisch Asien Herkunft; benötigt warmes, feuchtes Klima; Hauptanbau in Indien, China, und Südostasien.
preparationDe: Frisch oder getrocknet Rhizom Tee, candied ginger, powdered spice, standardisierte Extrakte.
cautionsDe: Generally recognized as safe als Nahrung; high medicinal doses kann interact mit anticoagulants—seek advice if on blood thinners.
floweringSeasonDe: Rarely Blüten in cultivation outside tropics
historicalNotesDe: Traded along ancient spice routes; among die earliest documented Asienn medicinal spices in Europaan records.

habitat: South Asia; tropical cultivation, especially India (major world producer).
traditionalUsesEn: Ayurvedic and South Asian culinary-medicinal use for digestion and inflammatory complaints; dye and ritual uses.
traditionalUsesDe: Ayurvedische und südasiatische Küchen- und Heilpflanze bei Verdauungs- und Entzündungsbeschwerden; Färbe- und Ritualgebrauch.
preparation: Powdered rhizome in food and golden milk, teas, standardized extracts (bioavailability varies; often with piperine or lipids).
cautions: Food amounts are generally safe. High-dose extracts may affect gallbladder conditions and drug metabolism—professional advice for therapeutic doses.
historicalNotes: Sanskrit and early Indian medical texts describe haridra; remains central to Indian cuisine and ritual.
habitatDe: Südasien; tropisch cultivation, especially Indien (major world producer).
preparationDe: Powdered Rhizom in food und golden milk, teas, standardisierte Extrakte (bioavailability varies; often mit piperine oder lipids).
cautionsDe: Nahrungsmengen are im Allgemeinen unbedenklich. High-dose Extrakte kann affect gallbladder conditions und drug metabolism—fachlicher Rat für therapeutic doses.
historicalNotesDe: Sanskrit und early Indienn medical texts describe haridra; remains central zu Indienn cuisine und ritual.

habitat: Cool temperate forests of Korea, northeastern China, and Russian Far East; extensively cultivated in Korea and China.
traditionalUsesEn: Traditional tonic for fatigue and reduced physical capacity; adaptogenic tradition in East Asian medicine.
traditionalUsesDe: Traditionelles Tonikum bei Müdigkeit und verminderter Leistungsfähigkeit; adaptogene Tradition der ostasiatischen Medizin.
preparation: Dried root decoctions, white/red ginseng products, standardized extracts.
cautions: May affect blood pressure, blood sugar, and interact with medications. Not for unrestricted long-term self-medication; avoid in certain hormonal conditions without advice.
historicalNotes: Name Panax from Greek "all-healing"; Korean red ginseng (steamed) is a traditional processed form.
habitatDe: Cool gemäßigt Wälder von Korea, northeastern China, und Russian Far East; extensively angebaut in Korea und China.
preparationDe: Getrocknet Wurzel Abkochungs, white/red ginseng products, standardisierte Extrakte.
cautionsDe: May affect Blutdruck, Blutzucker, und interact mit medications. Not für unrestricted long-term Selbstmedikation; vermeiden in certain hormonal conditions ohne Beratung.
historicalNotesDe: Name Panax von Greek "all-healing"; Korean red ginseng (steamed) is a traditionell processed form.

habitat: Native to East Asia; cultivated in China, India, Sri Lanka, Japan, and many tropical highlands.
traditionalUsesEn: Stimulant beverage (caffeine); traditional Chinese medicinal classifications vary by tea type (green, black, oolong, white).
traditionalUsesDe: Anregendes Getränk (Koffein); in der chinesischen Tradition je nach Teesorte (grün, schwarz, Oolong, weiß) unterschiedlich eingeordnet.
activeCompounds: Catechins (EGCG especially in green tea), caffeine, theanine, flavonoids
preparation: Infusion of fresh or oxidized leaves; matcha is stone-ground shade-grown green tea.
cautions: Caffeine content; high intake of green tea extracts has been linked to rare liver issues—food use is generally safe. Tannins may reduce iron absorption with meals.
historicalNotes: Chinese cultivation and use date back millennia; global trade shaped modern world history.
habitatDe: Heimisch in Ostasien; angebaut in China, Indien, Sri Lanka, Japan, und many tropisch highlands.
activeCompoundsDe: Catechins (EGCG especially in green Tee), caffeine, theanine, flavonoids
preparationDe: Aufguss von frisch oder oxidized Blätter; matcha is stone-ground shade-grown green Tee.
cautionsDe: Caffeine content; high intake von green Tee Extrakte has been linked zu selten liver issues—food use is im Allgemeinen unbedenklich. Tannins kann reduce iron absorption mit meals.
historicalNotesDe: Chinese cultivation und use date back millennia; global trade shaped modern world history.

partsUsed: Culinary stalk bases and leaves; essential oil from leaves
habitat: Tropical Asia origin; cultivated throughout the tropics, prominent in Thai and other Southeast Asian cuisines.
traditionalUsesEn: Culinary aromatic; traditional teas for mild digestive and feverish discomfort in folk practice; citral-rich essential oil used in fragrance and insect-repellent products.
traditionalUsesDe: Kulinarisches Aroma; traditionelle Tees bei leichten Verdauungs- und fiebrigen Beschwerden; citralreiches ätherisches Öl in Duft- und Insektenabwehrprodukten.
activeCompounds: Essential oil (citral: geranial and neral), myrcene, flavonoids
preparation: Fresh stalks in soups and teas; dried leaf tea; diluted essential oil external/aromatic use.
cautions: Essential oil must be diluted; may irritate skin. Culinary amounts generally safe.
historicalNotes: Distinct from East Indian lemongrass (C. flexuosus), which is more often used for oil production.
partsUsedDe: Kulinarisch stalk bases und Blätter; ätherisches Öl von Blätter
habitatDe: Tropisch Asien Herkunft; angebaut throughout die tropics, prominent in Thai und other Südostasienn cuisines.
activeCompoundsDe: Ätherisches Öl (citral: geranial und neral), myrcene, flavonoids
preparationDe: Frisch stalks in soups und teas; getrocknet Blatt Tee; verdünnt ätherisches Öl äußerlich/aromatic use.
cautionsDe: Ätherisches Öl muss verdünnt werden; kann irritate skin. Kulinarisch amounts im Allgemeinen unbedenklich.
historicalNotesDe: Distinct von East Indienn lemongrass (C. flexuosus), which is more often used für Öl production.

partsUsed: Aerial parts and/or root (depending on preparation and species)
habitat: North American prairies and open woodlands; native to central and eastern USA; widely cultivated.
traditionalUsesEn: Traditional Native American use for various infections and wounds; modern European herbal use for short-term support of common cold symptoms.
traditionalUsesDe: Traditionelle Nutzung durch indigene Völker Nordamerikas; in der europäischen Phytotherapie kurzzeitig unterstützend bei Erkältungssymptomen.
activeCompounds: Alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives (cichoric acid), polysaccharides, essential oil
preparation: Tinctures, pressed juice of aerial parts, dried herb/root teas and extracts.
cautions: Asteraceae allergy risk. Not recommended as prolonged continuous self-treatment; immune-related conditions need professional advice. Evidence for cold prevention/treatment is mixed.
historicalNotes: One of several Echinacea species used medicinally; E. purpurea is the most common cultivated ornamental and herbal species.
partsUsedDe: Kraut and/or Wurzel (depending on preparation und species)
habitatDe: Nordamerikan prairies und open Waldlands; heimisch in central und eastern USA; weit verbreitet angebaut.
activeCompoundsDe: Alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives (cichoric acid), polysaccharides, ätherisches Öl
preparationDe: Tinkturen, pressed juice von Kraut, getrocknet Kraut/Wurzel teas und Extrakte.
cautionsDe: Asteraceae Allergie risk. Nicht empfohlen as prolonged continuous Selbstbehandlung; immune-related conditions need fachlicher Rat. Evidence für cold prevention/treatment is mixed.
historicalNotesDe: One von several Echinacea species used medicinally; E. purpurea is die most common angebaut ornamental und herbal species.

habitat: Understory of tropical American rainforests; major cultivation in West Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia.
traditionalUsesEn: Mesoamerican ritual and beverage use; stimulant and food; traditional medicinal roles varied by culture.
traditionalUsesDe: Mesoamerikanische Ritual- und Getränkenutzung; Stimulans und Nahrungsmittel; medizinische Rollen je nach Kultur unterschiedlich.
activeCompounds: Theobromine, caffeine (smaller amounts), flavanols (epicatechin), fats (cocoa butter)
preparation: Fermented, dried, roasted beans for cocoa and chocolate; traditional frothed drinks with spices.
cautions: Stimulant content; high-calorie processed chocolate. Theobromine toxic to dogs and some pets.
historicalNotes: Name Theobroma means "food of the gods"; fundamental to Maya and Aztec culture before global chocolate trade.
habitatDe: Understory von tropisch American rainWälder; major cultivation in West Afrika, Latin America, und Südostasien.
activeCompoundsDe: Theobromine, caffeine (smaller amounts), flavanols (epicatechin), fats (cocoa butter)
preparationDe: Fermented, getrocknet, roasted beans für cocoa und chocolate; traditionell frothed drinks mit spices.
cautionsDe: Stimulant content; high-calorie processed chocolate. Theobromine toxic zu dogs und some pets.
historicalNotesDe: Name Theobroma means "food von die gods"; fundamental zu Maya und Aztec culture before global chocolate trade.

partsUsed: Leaf gel (inner parenchyma); latex (aloetic juice) from outer leaf is a different preparation
habitat: Likely Arabian Peninsula origin; naturalized and cultivated in warm dry regions worldwide including Mexico and North Africa.
traditionalUsesEn: Topical gel for minor burns and skin irritation; latex traditionally as strong laxative (not for casual use).
traditionalUsesDe: Gel äußerlich bei leichten Verbrennungen und Hautreizungen; Latex traditionell als starkes Abführmittel (nicht zur Selbstmedikation).
activeCompounds: Gel: polysaccharides (acemannan), glycoproteins. Latex: anthraquinone glycosides (aloin)
preparation: Fresh inner gel or stabilized gel products topically; latex products regulated and restricted in many markets.
cautions: Do not confuse gel with latex. Aloe latex is a stimulant laxative with safety concerns; oral non-decolorized whole-leaf extracts have been restricted in some jurisdictions. Patch-test topical products.
floweringSeason: Variable; tubular yellow flowers on tall racemes
historicalNotes: Depicted and described in ancient Egyptian and Greco-Roman sources; one of the most widely used topical houseplants.
partsUsedDe: Blatt gel (inner parenchyma); latex (aloetic juice) von outer Blatt is a different preparation
habitatDe: Likely Arabian Peninsula Herkunft; eingebürgert und angebaut in warm dry regions worldwide including Mexiko und North Afrika.
activeCompoundsDe: Gel: polysaccharides (acemannan), glycoproteins. Latex: anthraquinone glycosides (aloin)
preparationDe: Frisch inner gel oder stabilized gel products topically; latex products regulated und restricted in many markets.
cautionsDe: Do not confuse gel mit latex. Aloe latex is a stimulant laxative mit safety concerns; oral non-decolorized whole-Blatt Extrakte have been restricted in some jurisdictions. Patch-test äußerlich products.
floweringSeasonDe: Variable; tubular yellow Blüten on tall racemes
historicalNotesDe: Depicted und described in ancient Ägyptenian und Greco-Roman sources; one von die most widely used äußerlich houseplants.

habitat: Subtropical forests of southern South America (Argentina, Paraguay, Brazil, Uruguay); cultivated in plantations.
traditionalUsesEn: Traditional shared stimulant beverage (mate) of the Guaraní and later gaucho culture; social and daily tonic drink.
traditionalUsesDe: Traditionelles gemeinschaftliches Anregungsgetränk (Mate) der Guaraní und später der Gaucho-Kultur.
activeCompounds: Caffeine, theobromine, theophylline (smaller amounts), polyphenols, saponins
preparation: Dried leaves infused in a gourd (mate) with bombilla, or as tea bags / tereré (cold).
cautions: Caffeine content. Very hot repeated drinking of mate has been associated epidemiologically with increased esophageal cancer risk—temperature and lifestyle factors matter.
historicalNotes: Jesuit missions expanded cultivation in the 17th–18th centuries; national cultural drink in several South American countries.
habitatDe: Subtropisch Wälder von southern Südamerika (Argentina, Paraguay, Brasilien, Uruguay); angebaut in plantations.
activeCompoundsDe: Caffeine, theobromine, theophylline (smaller amounts), polyphenols, saponins
preparationDe: Getrocknet Blätter infused in a gourd (mate) mit bombilla, oder as Tee bags / tereré (cold).
cautionsDe: Caffeine content. Very hot repeated drinking von mate has been associated epidemiologically mit increased esophageal cancer risk—temperature und lifestyle factors matter.
historicalNotesDe: Jesuit missions expanded cultivation in die 17th–18th centuries; national cultural drink in several Südamerikan countries.

habitat: Southeastern United States and parts of Central/South America; disturbed ground, thickets; cultivated.
traditionalUsesEn: Traditional use for restlessness and difficulty sleeping; mild anxiety-related tension.
traditionalUsesDe: Traditionell bei Unruhe und Einschlafschwierigkeiten; leichte angstbedingte Anspannung.
activeCompounds: Flavonoids (vitexin, isovitexin), indole alkaloids (harman-type, in low amounts), maltol
preparation: Dried herb tea, tinctures, extracts in combination sleep/calm products.
cautions: May enhance sedative effects of other substances. Limited use in pregnancy without professional advice.
historicalNotes: Christian missionaries named the flower for symbols of the Passion; Native American and later European herbal use documented.
habitatDe: Southeastern Vereinigte Staaten und parts von Central/Südamerika; disturbed ground, thickets; angebaut.
activeCompoundsDe: Flavonoids (vitexin, isovitexin), indole alkaloids (harman-type, in low amounts), maltol
preparationDe: Getrocknet Kraut Tee, Tinkturen, Extrakte in combination sleep/calm products.
cautionsDe: May enhance sedative effects von other substances. Limited use in Schwangerschaft without fachlicher Rat.
historicalNotesDe: Christian missionaries named die Blüte für symbols von die Passion; Native American und later Europaan herbal use documented.

partsUsed: Needle-like leaves and stems (fermented or green)
habitat: Endemic to the Cederberg region of the Western Cape, South Africa (fynbos biome).
traditionalUsesEn: Traditional Khoisan and Cape beverage; modern caffeine-free tea alternative; folk use for mild digestive and allergic discomfort.
traditionalUsesDe: Traditionelles Getränk der Khoisan und der Kapregion; modernes koffeinfreies Tee-Getränk; volksmedizinisch bei leichten Verdauungs- und Allergiebeschwerden.
activeCompounds: Aspalathin and other dihydrochalcones, nothofagin, polyphenols; virtually caffeine-free
preparation: Oxidized (red) or unoxidized (green) leaf infusion.
cautions: Generally regarded as a safe beverage. Rare case reports of liver issues with excessive consumption—normal tea amounts are typical.
historicalNotes: Commercial cultivation expanded in the 20th century; GI-linked cultural product of South Africa.
partsUsedDe: Needle-like Blätter und Stängel (fermented oder green)
habitatDe: Endemic zu die Cederberg region von die Western Cape, Südafrika (fynbos biome).
activeCompoundsDe: Aspalathin und other dihydrochalcones, nothofagin, polyphenols; virtually caffeine-free
preparationDe: Oxidized (red) oder unoxidized (green) Blatt Aufguss.
cautionsDe: Generally regarded as a safe beverage. Selten case reports von liver issues mit excessive consumption—normal Tee amounts are typical.
historicalNotesDe: Commercial cultivation expanded in die 20th century; GI-linked cultural product von Südafrika.

scientificName: Boswellia sacra (related species also yield frankincense)
habitat: Arid rocky slopes of the Horn of Africa (Somalia) and southern Arabia (Oman, Yemen).
traditionalUsesEn: Incense in religious ritual; traditional use for inflammatory joint complaints (resin/extracts of Boswellia species); perfume and embalming historically.
traditionalUsesDe: Räucherwerk in religiösen Ritualen; traditionell bei entzündlichen Gelenkbeschwerden (Harz/Extrakte von Boswellia-Arten); historisch Parfüm und Einbalsamierung.
activeCompounds: Boswellic acids (in Boswellia resins), essential oil monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes
preparation: Burned as incense; resin extracts in modern herbal products (often B. serrata from India also used clinically).
cautions: Quality and species vary in trade. Internal extracts: professional guidance; possible gastrointestinal side effects and drug interactions.
floweringSeason: Unknown (variable; trees flower seasonally in arid habitats)
historicalNotes: One of the gifts in the Christian Nativity narrative; ancient incense trade linked Somalia and Arabia to Mediterranean empires.
habitatDe: Arid Felsenhänge von die Horn von Afrika (Somalia) und southern Arabia (Oman, Yemen).
activeCompoundsDe: Boswellic acids (in Boswellia resins), ätherisches Öl monoterpenes und sesquiterpenes
preparationDe: Burned as incense; Harz Extrakte in modern herbal products (often B. serrata von Indien also used clinically).
cautionsDe: Quality und species vary in trade. Internal Extrakte: fachlicher Anleitung; möglich gastrointestinal side effects und Arzneimittelwechselwirkungen.
floweringSeasonDe: Unbekannt (variable; trees Blüte seasonally in arid habitats)
historicalNotesDe: One von die gifts in die Christian Nativity narrative; ancient incense trade linked Somalia und Arabia zu Mittelmeergebiet empires.

partsUsed: Essential oil distilled from leaves and terminal branches
habitat: Coastal northeastern New South Wales and southeastern Queensland, Australia; swampy ground; cultivated for oil.
